title: lithocarpus caudatifolius
intro: Lithocarpus caudatifolius, also known as the Chinese tanbark oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to southern China. The medicinal properties of Lithocarpus caudatifolius include its bark, which contains tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and digestive issues. Lithocarpus caudatifolius can be prepared as a tea or decoction, and its bark may also be used to make tinctures or infusions for medicinal purposes.
